sn56.51:1.1:
1
Then the Blessed One took up a little bit of soil in his fingernail and addressed the bhikkhus thus:
sn56.51:1.2: "Bhikkhus, what do you think which is more: the little bit of soil that I have taken up in my fingernail or this great earth?"
sn56.51:1.3: "Venerable sir, the great earth is more. The little bit of soil that the Blessed One has taken up in his fingernail is trifling. Compared to the great earth, that little bit of soil is not calculable, does not bear comparison, does not amount even to a fraction."
sn56.51:1.4: "So too, bhikkhus, for a noble disciple, a person accomplished in view who has made the breakthrough, the suffering that has been destroyed and eliminated is more, while that which remains is trifling. Compared to the former mass of suffering that has been destroyed and eliminated, the latter is not calculable, does not bear comparison, does not amount even to a fraction, as there is a maximum of seven more lives. He is one who understands as it really is: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the way leading to the cessation of suffering.’
sn56.51:1.5:
2
"Therefore, bhikkhus, an exertion should be made to understand: ‘This is suffering.’ … An exertion should be made to understand: ‘This is the way leading to the cessation of suffering.’"
